title: cuphea ornithoides
intro: Cuphea ornithoides, also known as the bird flower or firecracker plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lythraceae. It is native to Central and South America and is known for its bright red, tubular flowers that resemble the shape of birds' beaks. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be beneficial for treating wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Cuphea ornithoides can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions that are said to promote digestive health, reduce fever, and boost the immune system.
